Steps.Message:
Sent by Will at 02:26 - I'm pleased to confirm that all power in the Orford Street area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected underground cable fault, which our engineers have diverted power away from.

We know that a power cut can be difficult, and I'm sorry for the length of the time you were without your electricity. Please rest assured that our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Will at 01:54 - In the meantime, our staff have been able to divert electricity around the faulty part of the network, restoring power to further customers. We know your power may now be back on, however; we'll keep sending text updates to everyone until all the power to your area is on. To stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Will at 01:50 - We are sorry to have to let you know that it's going to take us a little longer than we expected to get all the power back on in the area. Our engineers have advised the cabling is very close to a wall so they are having to take care with their excavations.

They are working to make a cut on the cable which will allow them to isolate the fault from the main network and restore power. This will take them a little more time to complete therefore, we're now aiming to restore supplies between 02:30 and 03:30. We're sorry we've had to change the estimated time, but we'll keep you updated.

Logs.Comment:
Sent by Stuart at 02:32 - We believe our engineers have been able to restore power to all 583 customers in the Wimbledon SW19 7 area by  diverting power around an underground cable fault. We're pleased that we have been able to restore power sooner than we estimated.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

We know that being without power can be worrying, but please rest assured that our staff work around the clock to keep the lights on. If you have any concerns or questions, give us a call any time.

Sent by Stuart at 02:26 - Thanks for your patience this morning.

Our staff are checking our equipment throughout the area to pinpoint the issue on our underground cabling network. In addition to this, they are liaising closely with our control room to find safe and reliable ways to divert power around any problems and get everyone restored.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them. We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Sent by Stuart at 02:08 - Our engineers have arrived on site in the Wimbledon SW19 7 area to investigate the power cut affecting 149 properties. 

Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off. 

We know that a power cut can be very disruptive, even more so if you are vulnerable. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress but for any concerns or questions, please call us free on 08003163105. For more information on how we support vulnerable customers just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR

Sent by Stuart at 01:26 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is an underground network fault that initially interrupted power to 583 properties.

However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 149 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment.

We're currently estimating power will be on between 02:30 and 03:30, but this may change depending on what our staff find.

Power back on? To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Otherwise, we'll keep you updated.
